paper_focus.title: Strong yet ductile low-density steel via multiphase heterogeneous microstructure
paper_focus.research_objective: To develop a low-density steel with a multiphase heterogeneous microstructure that simultaneously achieves high strength, good ductility, and effective stress concentration mitigation for lightweight structural applications through industrially feasible processing.
main_process_variables: vacuum induction melting and hot forging
hot rolling: reheating at 1200 °C/60 min, 6 passes from ~1100 °C to 880 °C, 90% reduction, air cooling
cold rolling: 60% reduction to 1.6 mm thickness
intercritical annealing at 750, 800, 850 °C for 30 min, air cooling
low bainite aging at 400 °C for 30 min
phase transformation temperatures: Ac1 = 705 °C, Ac3 = 950 °C, Bf = 380 °C, Ms = 346 °C
